summary: Independent read-only QA accepts M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-SCHEMA-001 at branch head 50540c832f882ea647b42678d83f2b7da235c7b2. The implemented schema and query path define an authoritative MapTopologyDefinitionV1 with district polygons, anchors, metadata, route nodes, explicit route endpoints/edges, route modes, capacity, seasonality, availability, terrain/risk/historicity metadata, and stable topology hash. sim.list-map-topology and sim.preview-map-topology-path reject missing topology and derive reachability/cost only from explicit topology.routeEdges; visual coordinates, row/column, hex, sequential ids, renderer geometry, and hidden regular lattice shortcuts are not used in the new topology path. Save/load preserves topology hash and rejects missing or mismatched topology for topology-backed runtimes. Scope changes are within the task's allowed paths; the mid-task anti-grid amendment is recorded in the task file, ADR, PROJECT_STATUS, downstream task criteria, and lead handoff without authorizing client rendering, content-source migration, final art, manual node battle, server, telemetry, or formal historical map claims.

tests_run.result: Clean before QA handoff creation at branch codex/m7-map-topology-schema tracking origin/codex/m7-map-topology-schema.

tests_run.command: git diff --name-status origin/main...HEAD and git diff --name-status
tests_run.result: Committed diff only touches PROJECT_STATUS, ADR, sim-core, protocol, save-format, allowed task/state/message files, and tests. No forbidden apps/web, apps/desktop, packages/ui, packages/map-renderer, content-source, .github, tools, gate, roadmap, manifest, checksum, or validation-report paths. No uncommitted changes before QA handoff creation.

PASS: 26 test files and 198 tests passed, including tests/sim-core-map-topology.test.ts anti-grid coverage for visual-neighbor-without-route, visually-distant explicit route, geometry perturbation without path change, stable route tie-break evidence, topology hash validation, and no-topology query rejection.
PASS: 1 test file and 14 tests passed, including sim.list-map-topology and sim.preview-map-topology-path query schema coverage.
PASS: 3 test files and 29 tests passed, including topology save/load hash preservation plus missing and mismatched topology rejection.
PASS: 1 test file and 16 tests passed.
PASS: format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.

decisions.decision: Accept the schema/query/save implementation because topology-backed pathing uses explicit topology.routeEdges and missing topology is rejected on the new query path instead of falling back to legacy grid/route behavior.

Accept the mid-task anti-grid amendment as properly recorded and in scope: schema tests prove visual-neighbor-without-route, visually-distant explicit route, geometry perturbation without path change, and stable tie-break behavior.

Allow definitions.routes/M2 transport compatibility as a projection during topology introduction because topology-backed previews delegate to previewMapTopologyPathV1 before projecting to the legacy M2 read shape; old no-topology worlds retain legacy behavior only outside the new authoritative topology path.
This ACCEPT does not approve M7 content lock or M7 exit; downstream fixture, client, and validation tasks must still complete and the product owner must explicitly reconsider content lock.

risks.summary: The default player-facing content and client renderer are intentionally not migrated in this schema task; the product-facing map may still present old fixture/client behavior until M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-FIXTURE-001 and M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-CLIENT-001 land.
risks.mitigation: Downstream task acceptance criteria now require irregular COMPOSITE fixture topology, anti-grid/lattice proof, topology read-model driven rendering, and screenshot/component evidence.

risks.summary: sim-core schema validation checks topology references, duplicate ids/source ids, seasonality, hash, and polygon point count, but zero-area/self-intersection polygon rejection and route-node-inside-polygon enforcement are deferred to fixture/content validation work.
risks.mitigation: M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-FIXTURE-001 and M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-VALIDATION-001 now explicitly require negative content-compiler tests for invalid polygons, route nodes outside polygons, disconnected graphs, and grid/hex/lattice leakage.

risks.summary: definitions.routes still exists as a compatibility projection/migration bridge and M2 preview still supports legacy no-topology worlds.
risks.mitigation: Topology-backed worlds delegate M2 preview to topology.routeEdges first; QA should keep this risk visible until fixture/client/migration tasks remove player-facing dependence on legacy routes.

risks.summary: M7 content lock remains blocked with content_lock_accepted=false; this schema ACCEPT is not product-owner approval.
risks.mitigation: Do not start M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 until the full topology recovery chain validates in origin/main and the product owner reconsiders content lock.

requested_action: Lead orchestrator should integrate the accepted schema review through the normal PR/CI/main flow, keep the carried fixture/client/content-validation risks on the downstream topology tasks, and not reopen M7 content lock or M7 exit until the full topology recovery chain is accepted.
